title: GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) Compliance Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
| inputlookup gdpr_scc_register.csv
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(signed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval scc_age_days=round((now()-signed_epoch)/86400,0)
| lookup gdpr_processor_register.csv processor_name OUTPUT sub_processors_last_changed
| eval sub_change_epoch=strptime(sub_processors_last_changed,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval review_needed=if(sub_change_epoch > signed_epoch OR scc_age_days > 1095, 1, 0)
| where review_needed=1 OR scc_version!="2021"
| table processor_name, scc_version, signed_date, scc_age_days, sub_processors_last_changed, review_needed
value: SCCs must be implemented and kept current with module and annex accuracy. This use case monitors SCC version, signing dates, linked processing activities, and triggers review when processor subprocessors or transfer countries change materially.
implementation: (1) Record each SCC with version (2021 modules) and signing date; (2) sync sub-processor change dates from vendor notifications; (3) alert legal when sub-processor changes post-date the SCC signature; (4) schedule triennial SCC review tasks; (5) archive signed PDFs externally with URLs in register.
visualization: Table (SCCs needing review), Single value (outdated SCC count), Timeline (signature vs sub-processor changes), Bar chart (by module type).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
